prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
German:
"Der beste Zeitpunkt, um eine Eiche zu pflanzen, war vor hundert Jahren. Der zweitbeste Zeitpunkt ist in diesem Moment."
Note: In this German rendition, the original sentiment is maintained but with an oak tree (Eiche) instead of a generic tree. Oaks are often associated with longevity and strength in German culture.

French: 
"Il aurait fallu commencer à écrire son livre ou à construire sa maison il y a dix ans. Mais il n'est jamais trop tard pour commencer maintenant."
Note: The French translation shifts the metaphor from planting a tree to other personal endeavors like writing a book or building a house. The core message of acting in the present, even if it feels long overdue, is preserved.
